chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: رِوَايَةِ حَرِيزٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا اِغْتَسَلَ اَلْمُحْرِمُ مِنَ اَلْجَنَابَةِ صَبَّ عَلَى رَأْسِهِ اَلْمَاءَ وَ يَمِيزُ اَلشَّعْرَ بِأَنَامِلِهِ بَعْضَهُ مِنْ بَعْضٍ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2707 - In the narration of Hariz from Abu Abdullah (as), Imam (as) said:
"When a person in the state of ihram performs ghusl (ritual bath) for janabah (major ritual impurity), they should pour water over their head and separate their hair with their fingers, parting it gently."
